payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: fix_extraction.md
+# Fix Extraction: Visual And Macro Understanding Recovery Plan
+
+Status: proposed implementation plan based on the 2026-07-03 rerun of
+`Pop Creations Flow 12112025 (1).png`.
+
+Target document ID: `9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a`.
+
+Latest comparison artifact:
+
+- `.cache/macro-comparison/9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a/rerun-20260703T022732Z/`
+
+## Executive Summary
+
+The 2026-07-03 visual/macro rerun proved that the new visual prompt and lens
+fan-out can produce more atomic claims, but it did not prove better holistic
+understanding.
+
+Observed result:
+
+- Old live snapshot before reset: 242 claims.
+- New live snapshot after rerun: 307 claims.
+- New source outlines: 1.
+- New macro relationships: 0.
+- New coverage findings: 0.
+- Exact summary overlap: 4.
+- The comparison verdict was `mostly_increased_atomic_volume`.
+- 127 new claims were labeled `policy`, which is too high for a visual process
+  map where ordinary arrows should usually be `observed_practice` or
+  `uncertain`.
+
+The system failed in two broad ways:
+
+1. Macro/coverage mechanics failed: follow-up tasks had SQL projection bugs and
+   then model-output schema failures.
+2. Extraction quality failed: lens fan-out amplified atomic claims without
+   durable visual relationship identity, strong deduplication, or deterministic
+   claim-kind discipline.
+
+The fix is not just prompt tuning. The pipeline needs staged orchestration,
+tolerant structured-output parsing, pre-promotion deduplication, deterministic
+visual claim-kind gates, and eventually durable visual context with stable
+relationship IDs.
+
+## Goals
+
+For dense visual workflow sources, the pipeline should produce:
+
+- A faithful visual rendering that remains quote-validating evidence.
+- A durable provisional visual context with element and relationship identity.
+- A controlled set of atomic claims, close to one claim per meaningful visual
+  relationship.
+- Few or no duplicate claims from broad extraction plus lens fan-out.
+- Claim kinds that distinguish visual sequence from official policy.
+- Macro relationships that summarize workflow structure from validated claims.
+- Coverage findings when the source outline identifies important structure that
+  claims or macro relationships do not cover.
+
+## Non-Goals
+
+- Do not weaken exact quote validation.
+- Do not let source outlines or visual context become approved evidence by
+  themselves.
+- Do not manually delete production knowledge rows outside the guarded
+  re-evaluation path, except as a documented emergency fallback.
+- Do not make visual extraction depend on this one POP flowchart's layout or
+  business vocabulary.
+
+## Phase 1: Stabilize Macro And Coverage Output Parsing
+
+### Problem
+
+The macro relationship retry failed because the model did not return the exact
+expected top-level `relationships` array. Coverage audit failed because the
+model returned malformed/aliased finding fields. These are model-output-shape
+failures, not necessarily reasoning failures.
+
+Relevant files:
+
+- `packages/ai/src/prompts/macro-relationship.ts`
+- `packages/ai/src/prompts/coverage-audit.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/macro-relationship-extraction.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-coverage-audit.ts`
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Add a tolerant macro relationship schema preprocessor.
+   - Accept wrappers:
+     - `relationships`
+     - `macroRelationships`
+     - `macro_relationships`
+     - `result.relationships`
+     - `data.relationships`
+     - `output.relationships`
+   - Normalize relationship type aliases:
+     - `process_sequence` -> `sequence`
+     - `approval_gate` -> `dependency` or `handoff`
+     - `handoff_chain` -> `handoff`
+     - `exception_branch` -> `exception_path`
+     - `rework_loop` -> `exception_path`
+     - `policy_practice_tension` -> `policy_vs_practice_tension`
+   - Normalize support claim fields:
+     - `claimIds` -> `supportClaimIds`
+     - `supportClaims` -> `supportClaimIds` when values are IDs
+     - `evidenceClaimIds` -> `supportClaimIds`
+   - Default `relationships` to `[]` only when the output clearly represents an
+     empty result, not when support fields are malformed.
+   - Preserve strict validation that every support claim ID exists in the
+     provided claim set.
+
+2. Add a tolerant coverage audit schema preprocessor.
+   - Accept wrappers:
+     - `findings`
+     - `coverageFindings`
+     - `coverage_findings`
+     - `result.findings`
+     - `data.findings`
+     - `output.findings`
+   - Normalize finding type aliases:
+     - `missing_process_stage` -> `missing_stage`
+     - `missing_role` -> `missing_owner`
+     - `missing_decision_branch` -> `missing_branch`
+     - `uncovered_exception` -> `unrepresented_exception`
+     - `unrepresented_stage` -> `missing_stage`
+     - `not_represented` -> `low_claim_density`
+   - Normalize action/recommendation aliases into the current enum.
+   - Drop only unrecoverable individual findings, not the whole output, when
+     one finding is malformed.
+
+3. Add one structured-output repair retry.
+   - On Zod failure, call the same route once with:
+     - original raw model output
+     - schema error summary
+     - strict instruction to return only corrected JSON
+   - Record the repair attempt in `model_run_attempts`.
+   - Preserve the original failure for audit.
+
+4. Add no-network verification scripts.
+   - `packages/ai/src/__verify__/macro-relationship-schema.ts`
+   - `packages/ai/src/__verify__/coverage-audit-schema.ts`
+   - Include wrapper, alias, empty-array, malformed-enum, and unrecoverable
+     cases.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Macro schema accepts reasonable wrappers and aliases.
+- Coverage schema accepts reasonable wrappers and aliases.
+- Invalid support claim IDs still fail.
+- A bad single coverage finding does not discard otherwise valid findings.
+- Verification scripts pass locally.
+
+## Phase 2: Fix Follow-Up Orchestration Order
+
+### Problem
+
+`source-outline` currently triggers lens fan-out, macro extraction, and coverage
+audit at roughly the same time. Macro and coverage therefore do not wait for
+lens results. Even a successful macro pass can miss the richer post-lens claim
+set.
+
+Relevant files:
+
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-outline.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/macro-relationship-extraction.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-coverage-audit.ts`
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Add a `document-macro-finalize` task.
+   - Payload:
+     - `documentId`
+     - `sourceOutlineId`
+     - optional `expectedLensRunCount`
+   - Responsibilities:
+     - Detect whether all lens runs for the outline have completed, failed, or
+       timed out.
+     - Trigger macro relationship extraction after lens fan-out settles.
+     - Trigger coverage audit after macro extraction completes or reaches a
+       terminal failure.
+
+2. Stop `source-outline` from directly triggering macro and coverage.
+   - It should trigger lens fan-out and then the finalizer.
+   - If zero lenses are triggered, finalizer can run immediately.
+   - If lenses are triggered, finalizer should use either delayed retry or DB
+     state to wait.
+
+3. Track lens follow-up state.
+   - Preferred: add a small `document_lens_runs` or `source_outline_followups`
+     table.
+   - Minimum viable: use `job_runs` keyed by `sourceOutlineId`, but this is
+     less explicit.
+   - Store:
+     - `source_outline_id`
+     - `source_group_id`
+     - `lens`
+     - `trigger_run_id`
+     - `status`
+     - `claims_promoted`
+     - `duplicates_appended`
+     - `finished_at`
+
+4. Make coverage audit run last.
+   - Coverage should see:
+     - source outline
+     - broad extraction claims
+     - lens extraction claims
+     - macro relationships if they exist
+     - macro failure metadata if macro extraction failed
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Macro relationship extraction starts only after lens fan-out reaches terminal
+  state.
+- Coverage audit starts only after macro relationship extraction succeeds or
+  fails terminally.
+- The finalizer is idempotent and safe to retry.
+- Rerun artifacts show the full ordered sequence.
+
+## Phase 3: Add Deterministic Visual Claim-Kind Discipline
+
+### Problem
+
+The rerun produced 127 `policy` claims from a visual flowchart. The prompt says
+a bare arrow is not policy, but the model still upgraded procedural-looking
+sequence into official policy.
+
+Relevant files:
+
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
+- `packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts`
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Add a deterministic visual policy gate.
+   - Apply when:
+     - source is image/visual
+     - visual graph mode is detected
+     - model emits `claimKind = policy`
+   - Preserve `policy` only when the exact quote or nearby rendering contains
+     explicit policy evidence.
+
+2. Define explicit policy evidence terms.
+   - Examples:
+     - `policy`
+     - `SOP`
+     - `standard operating`
+     - `required`
+     - `must`
+     - `shall`
+     - `authorization`
+     - `approval required`
+     - `compliance`
+   - A box named `Approval` can support approval-gate meaning, but not
+     automatically `policy`.
+
+3. Downgrade unsupported visual policy labels.
+   - If visual arrow/edge lacks explicit policy evidence:
+     - set `claimKind = observed_practice` or `uncertain`
+     - cap `claimKindConfidence` at 6 for observed practice
+     - cap `claimKindConfidence` at 5 for uncertain
+   - Store metadata:
+     - `claimKindOriginal`
+     - `claimKindDowngradeReason`
+     - `visualPolicyGateApplied: true`
+
+4. Apply the same gate in broad document ingestion and document lens extraction.
+
+5. Add tests.
+   - Bare arrow fixture should not produce policy.
+   - Explicit `required approval` fixture may produce policy.
+   - `Approval` box alone should not force policy.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Visual diagram reruns do not generate large numbers of `policy` claims unless
+  the source text visibly supports them.
+- Downgrade decisions are visible in metadata.
+- Admin reviewers can still revise kind manually.
+
+## Phase 4: Deduplicate Before Promotion
+
+### Problem
+
+Lens fan-out promoted many repeated claims. The comparison showed exact summary
+duplicates and likely near-duplicates. Deduplication must happen before claims
+hit the review queue.
+
+Relevant files:
+
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
+- `packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/promote-candidate.ts`
+- candidate hash helpers under `packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/`
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Add same-document exact summary dedup.
+   - Normalize summary:
+     - lowercase
+     - trim punctuation
+     - collapse whitespace
+   - Within same document and same source batch family, keep one candidate.
+
+2. Add same-quote plus same-claim-type dedup.
+   - If candidates share:
+     - document ID
+     - normalized exact quote
+     - claim type
+   - Keep best candidate by confidence/impact or earliest broad extraction
+     candidate.
+
+3. Add cross-type visual duplicate resolution.
+   - If same quote produces multiple claim types:
+     - prefer `exception_rule` for conditional branch
+     - prefer `dependency` for simple sequence
+     - prefer `process_rule` only when source states an actual rule
+   - Drop or mark weaker variants as duplicates.
+
+4. Add bounded fuzzy dedup.
+   - Scope only:
+     - same document
+     - same source chunk
+     - same or highly similar quote
+   - Use token/Jaccard similarity first.
+   - Consider embeddings only inside a small candidate set.
+
+5. Record duplicate outcomes.
+   - In validation results:
+     - `duplicate_same_summary`
+     - `duplicate_same_quote_same_type`
+     - `duplicate_cross_type_visual_edge`
+     - `near_duplicate_same_source`
+   - In job output:
+     - `duplicatesCollapsedBeforePromotion`
+     - `crossTypeDuplicatesResolved`
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Lens fan-out no longer multiplies repeated claims for the same visual edge.
+- Duplicate candidates remain auditable.
+- Existing R5 quote validation remains unchanged.
+
+## Phase 5: Make Lens Fan-Out Focused
+
+### Problem
+
+The rerun's lens output behaved like repeated broad extraction. Four handoff
+lens runs promoted 249 lens claims. Lens passes need to target missing coverage,
+not restate broad process edges.
+
+Relevant files:
+
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-outline.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
+- `apps/workers/src/lib/document-lens-budget.ts`
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Pass existing claims into lens prompts.
+   - Include broad extraction claims for the same document/group.
+   - Include earlier lens claims for the same outline.
+   - Prompt instruction:
+     - Do not restate any listed claims.
+     - Only extract missing facts for this lens.
+
+2. Limit lens input to the group.
+   - Use `source_group_items` to select group-specific chunks.
+   - If every group references all chunks, improve source-outline grouping.
+   - Avoid passing the full visual rendering to every lens unless necessary.
+
+3. Enforce lens diversity.
+   - Do not let all selected lens runs be `handoffs`.
+   - Prefer a mix:
+     - `handoffs`
+     - `exceptions_and_workarounds`
+     - `dependencies_and_sequence`
+     - `systems_and_data_entry`
+   - No more than one lens per group by default.
+
+4. Add per-lens extraction criteria.
+   - `handoffs`: only ownership/role handoffs.
+   - `exceptions_and_workarounds`: only branches, loops, fail/pass paths.
+   - `systems_and_data_entry`: only system touchpoints/data movement.
+   - `dependencies_and_sequence`: only missing sequence dependencies.
+
+5. Skip covered groups.
+   - Before triggering a lens, compare source group against existing claims.
+   - Skip if broad extraction already covers the group.
+   - Store skip reason in outline budget metadata.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Lens fan-out increases coverage without producing a claim explosion.
+- Job output shows skipped groups and reasons.
+- The same relationship is not extracted by broad, handoff, and dependency
+  passes as separate claims.
+
+## Phase 6: Add Durable Visual Context
+
+### Problem
+
+The system still treats a visual graph as anonymous text. Without durable
+relationship IDs, each worker can rediscover the same edge with different words.
+
+Relevant files:
+
+- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
+- `packages/ai/src/prompts/visual-understanding.ts`
+- `packages/db/src/schema.ts`
+- new migration under `packages/db/migrations/sql/`
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Add `document_visual_contexts`.
+   - Suggested columns:
+     - `id`
+     - `document_id`
+     - `model_run_id`
+     - `context_pack_id`
+     - `prompt_version`
+     - `artifact_type`
+     - `artifact_type_confidence`
+     - `visual_grammar_json`
+     - `elements_json`
+     - `relationships_json`
+     - `regions_json`
+     - `uncertainties_json`
+     - `final_rendering_text`
+     - `status`
+     - `created_at`
+
+2. Add stable relationship IDs.
+   - Each relationship should contain:
+     - `relationshipId`
+     - source element ID
+     - target element ID
+     - label/condition
+     - inferred relationship type
+     - owner/group/region
+     - direction confidence
+     - uncertainty notes
+     - rendering line/span
+
+3. Persist final rendering as chunks.
+   - Existing quote validation still uses `document_chunks.raw_text`.
+   - Chunk metadata should record:
+     - `visualContextId`
+     - relationship IDs covered by the chunk
+     - element IDs covered by the chunk
+
+4. Preserve visual IDs on candidates and claims.
+   - Candidate metadata:
+     - `visualContextId`
+     - `visualRelationshipIds`
+     - `visualElementIds`
+     - `visualRegionIds`
+   - Claim metadata after promotion should preserve the same IDs.
+
+5. Dedup by relationship ID.
+   - Default rule:
+     - one distinct visual relationship generally produces one claim.
+   - Multiple claims from one relationship require distinct claim type and
+     distinct supported meaning.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Every visual claim can be traced to both a quote span and a visual
+  relationship ID.
+- Deduplication can identify the same edge across broad and lens passes.
+- Source outline can consume structured visual context instead of inferring all
+  visual structure from prose.
+
+## Phase 7: Add Visual Faithfulness Validation
+
+### Problem
+
+Current validation proves a claim quotes the transcription. It does not prove
+the transcription matches the image.
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Add deterministic visual context validation.
+   - Every relationship references existing element IDs.
+   - Every relationship has a rendering span.
+   - No duplicate relationship IDs.
+   - Direction confidence is present.
+   - Uncertainty notes exist when confidence is low.
+   - Final rendering is non-empty and chunkable.
+
+2. Add optional self-verification.
+   - Input:
+     - original image
+     - final rendering
+     - structured visual context
+   - Ask only:
+     - missing elements
+     - wrong endpoints
+     - wrong direction
+     - wrong grouping/owner
+     - unsupported relationship
+     - dropped labels
+   - Do not let self-verification directly create approved facts.
+
+3. Add eval fixtures.
+   - POP flow image.
+   - Org chart.
+   - Table screenshot.
+   - App screenshot/form.
+   - Non-swimlane process diagram.
+
+4. Add metrics.
+   - Element recall/precision.
+   - Relationship recall/precision.
+   - Endpoint accuracy.
+   - Direction accuracy.
+   - Owner/group accuracy.
+   - Branch label accuracy.
+   - Duplicate ratio.
+   - Claim kind error rate.
+   - Quote validation pass rate.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Prompt/model changes can be compared against ground truth.
+- Future model swaps cannot silently regress visual faithfulness.
+
+## Phase 8: Improve Macro Relationship Quality
+
+### Problem
+
+Even after schema parsing is fixed, macro extraction needs curated support
+claims and better prompts to avoid weak summaries.
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Build support claim clusters.
+   - Approval gates.
+   - Rework loops.
+   - Buyer/customer branches.
+   - Licensor branches.
+   - System/data handoffs.
+   - Production/shipping path.
+
+2. Feed macro extraction curated clusters, not arbitrary top 40 claims.
+
+3. Ask for relationship types tied to graph structure.
+   - Sequence backbone.
+   - Approval gate.
+   - Exception path.
+   - Cross-role handoff chain.
+   - System handoff chain.
+
+4. Require minimum support.
+   - Usually 2+ support claims.
+   - Allow one support claim only for a simple source-local sequence backbone
+     when evidence is unambiguous.
+
+5. Add macro duplicate prevention.
+   - Same outline + relationship type + similar summary should not create
+     multiple rows.
+
+6. Improve prompt examples.
+   - Include valid output examples.
+   - Include `{"relationships":[]}` no-op example.
+   - Emphasize support claim IDs exactly as provided.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Process diagrams with hundreds of claims produce useful macro relationships.
+- Macro rows are pending/reviewable, not silently absent.
+- Weak or duplicate macro summaries are filtered before persistence.
+
+## Phase 9: Make Coverage Audit A Real Safety Net
+
+### Problem
+
+When macro extraction fails or produces nothing, coverage audit should create
+actionable findings. It should not also silently fail or return zero rows.
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Compare outline groups against claims.
+   - For every source group:
+     - count supporting claims.
+     - count macro relationships.
+     - detect missing owner/branch/stage coverage.
+
+2. Add deterministic fallback findings.
+   - If model coverage audit fails:
+     - create an admin-only finding that coverage audit failed.
+   - If macro relationships are zero for a rich outline:
+     - create `low_claim_density` or `macro_only_source` as appropriate.
+
+3. Consider adding internal finding types.
+   - `macro_relationship_generation_failed`
+   - `duplicate_atomic_volume`
+   - `policy_kind_overuse`
+
+4. Add coverage metrics to job output.
+   - Groups checked.
+   - Claims per group.
+   - Relationships per group.
+   - Findings generated.
+   - Model findings dropped during schema normalization.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- A rich visual outline with no macro relationships produces at least one
+  coverage finding.
+- Coverage audit failure is visible in admin artifacts and job output.
+
+## Phase 10: Harden Re-Evaluation Tooling
+
+### Problem
+
+`scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs` initially failed because macro/source-outline
+refs still pointed at chunks. The script must stay current with the macro
+schema.
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Keep source-outline cleanup in the guarded script.
+   - Delete document-tied:
+     - `source_coverage_findings`
+     - `source_group_items`
+     - `source_groups`
+     - `source_outline_source_refs`
+     - `source_outline_sources`
+     - `source_outlines`
+
+2. Include macro cleanup.
+   - Delete document/claim/outline-tied:
+     - `macro_relationship_review_events`
+     - `macro_relationship_claims`
+     - `macro_relationship_sources`
+     - `macro_relationships`
+
+3. Add extra blockers.
+   - Approved macro relationships tied to target claims.
+   - Macro relationships consumed by Brain/chat once such lineage exists.
+
+4. Expand dry-run output.
+   - Print:
+     - chunks
+     - claims
+     - candidates
+     - batches
+     - outlines
+     - groups
+     - macro relationships
+     - coverage findings
+
+5. Add a smoke fixture or script.
+   - Document with chunks plus source outline refs should reset without FK
+     errors.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Re-evaluation does not FK fail when source outlines exist.
+- Dry run makes all affected artifact classes visible.
+- Approved macro knowledge cannot be accidentally deleted.
+
+## Phase 11: Improve Artifact And Metrics Export
+
+### Problem
+
+The comparison artifact was useful, but some metrics were hard to interpret.
+Historical job rows mixed with current rerun rows, and quote-overlap metrics
+need to read the exact evidence column reliably.
+
+### Tasks
+
+1. Fix quote extraction in export helper.
+   - Use actual `claim_evidence` quote field names.
+   - Verify normalized quote overlap is measuring real quote text.
+
+2. Separate run windows.
+   - Export:
+     - all historical rows
+     - current rerun rows only
+   - Use explicit file names:
+     - `rerun-job-outputs-after-rerun.json`
+     - `historical-job-outputs.json`
+
+3. Add model/prompt metadata.
+   - Vision route.
+   - Vision prompt version.
+   - Extraction route.
+   - Extraction prompt version.
+   - Source outline prompt version.
+   - Macro prompt version.
+   - Coverage prompt version.
+
+4. Add quality metrics.
+   - Claims per visual relationship once available.
+   - Duplicate collapsed count.
+   - Policy downgrade count.
+   - Macro rows persisted.
+   - Coverage findings persisted.
+
+### Acceptance Criteria
+
+- Comparison summaries distinguish real claim loss from changed rendering
+  wording.
+- Rerun artifacts are reproducible without manual DB archaeology.
+
+## Phase 12: Rerun Gate And Rollout
+
+### Pre-Rerun Gates
+
+Run before production-like resets:
+
+- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/visual-understanding-prompt.ts`
+- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/source-outline-schema.ts`
+- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/macro-relationship-schema.ts`
+- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/coverage-audit-schema.ts`
+- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai run typecheck`
+- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run typecheck`
+
+### Production-Like Rerun Thresholds
+
+For this POP flow image:
+
+- New claim count should not rise more than 20 percent unless duplicate collapse
+  explains the increase.
+- Visual `policy` claims should be rare and justified by explicit source text.
+- Macro relationships should be nonzero.
+- Coverage findings should be nonzero if macro relationships are zero.
+- Duplicate summary groups should be near zero.
+- Lens claims should add missing coverage, not restate broad extraction.
+
+### Rollout Order
+
+1. Tolerant macro/coverage schemas and repair retry.
+2. Staged finalizer after lens fan-out.
+3. Deterministic visual policy downgrade.
+4. Pre-promotion deduplication.
+5. Focused lens prompts and budget diversity.
+6. Durable visual context and relationship IDs.
+7. Visual faithfulness eval harness.
+8. Rerun the POP flow document and compare again.
+
+## Final Diagnosis
+
+The visual prompt gave the system a better textual substrate, but downstream
+workers still treated that substrate as anonymous prose. The pipeline therefore
+created more atomic claims instead of stable process understanding.
+
+The durable fix is to give visual relationships identity, make every extraction
+pass respect that identity, and run macro/coverage only after the atomic layer
+has settled.
*** End Patch

# Fix Extraction: Visual And Macro Understanding Recovery Plan

Status: proposed implementation plan based on the 2026-07-03 rerun of
`Pop Creations Flow 12112025 (1).png`.

Target document ID: `9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a`.

Latest comparison artifact:

- `.cache/macro-comparison/9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a/rerun-20260703T022732Z/`

## Executive Summary

The 2026-07-03 visual/macro rerun proved that the new visual prompt and lens
fan-out can produce more atomic claims, but it did not prove better holistic
understanding.

Observed result:

- Old live snapshot before reset: 242 claims.
- New live snapshot after rerun: 307 claims.
- New source outlines: 1.
- New macro relationships: 0.
- New coverage findings: 0.
- Exact summary overlap: 4.
- The comparison verdict was `mostly_increased_atomic_volume`.
- 127 new claims were labeled `policy`, which is too high for a visual process
  map where ordinary arrows should usually be `observed_practice` or
  `uncertain`.

The system failed in two broad ways:

1. Macro/coverage mechanics failed: follow-up tasks had SQL projection bugs and
   then model-output schema failures.
2. Extraction quality failed: lens fan-out amplified atomic claims without
   durable visual relationship identity, strong deduplication, or deterministic
   claim-kind discipline.

The fix is not just prompt tuning. The pipeline needs staged orchestration,
tolerant structured-output parsing, pre-promotion deduplication, deterministic
visual claim-kind gates, and eventually durable visual context with stable
relationship IDs.

## Goals

For dense visual workflow sources, the pipeline should produce:

- A faithful visual rendering that remains quote-validating evidence.
- A durable provisional visual context with element and relationship identity.
- A controlled set of atomic claims, close to one claim per meaningful visual
  relationship.
- Few or no duplicate claims from broad extraction plus lens fan-out.
- Claim kinds that distinguish visual sequence from official policy.
- Macro relationships that summarize workflow structure from validated claims.
- Coverage findings when the source outline identifies important structure that
  claims or macro relationships do not cover.

## Non-Goals

- Do not weaken exact quote validation.
- Do not let source outlines or visual context become approved evidence by
  themselves.
- Do not manually delete production knowledge rows outside the guarded
  re-evaluation path, except as a documented emergency fallback.
- Do not make visual extraction depend on this one POP flowchart's layout or
  business vocabulary.

## Phase 1: Stabilize Macro And Coverage Output Parsing

### Problem

The macro relationship retry failed because the model did not return the exact
expected top-level `relationships` array. Coverage audit failed because the
model returned malformed/aliased finding fields. These are model-output-shape
failures, not necessarily reasoning failures.

Relevant files:

- `packages/ai/src/prompts/macro-relationship.ts`
- `packages/ai/src/prompts/coverage-audit.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/macro-relationship-extraction.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-coverage-audit.ts`

### Tasks

1. Add a tolerant macro relationship schema preprocessor.
   - Accept wrappers:
     - `relationships`
     - `macroRelationships`
     - `macro_relationships`
     - `result.relationships`
     - `data.relationships`
     - `output.relationships`
   - Normalize relationship type aliases:
     - `process_sequence` -> `sequence`
     - `approval_gate` -> `dependency` or `handoff`
     - `handoff_chain` -> `handoff`
     - `exception_branch` -> `exception_path`
     - `rework_loop` -> `exception_path`
     - `policy_practice_tension` -> `policy_vs_practice_tension`
   - Normalize support claim fields:
     - `claimIds` -> `supportClaimIds`
     - `supportClaims` -> `supportClaimIds` when values are IDs
     - `evidenceClaimIds` -> `supportClaimIds`
   - Default `relationships` to `[]` only when the output clearly represents an
     empty result, not when support fields are malformed.
   - Preserve strict validation that every support claim ID exists in the
     provided claim set.

2. Add a tolerant coverage audit schema preprocessor.
   - Accept wrappers:
     - `findings`
     - `coverageFindings`
     - `coverage_findings`
     - `result.findings`
     - `data.findings`
     - `output.findings`
   - Normalize finding type aliases:
     - `missing_process_stage` -> `missing_stage`
     - `missing_role` -> `missing_owner`
     - `missing_decision_branch` -> `missing_branch`
     - `uncovered_exception` -> `unrepresented_exception`
     - `unrepresented_stage` -> `missing_stage`
     - `not_represented` -> `low_claim_density`
   - Normalize action/recommendation aliases into the current enum.
   - Drop only unrecoverable individual findings, not the whole output, when
     one finding is malformed.

3. Add one structured-output repair retry.
   - On Zod failure, call the same route once with:
     - original raw model output
     - schema error summary
     - strict instruction to return only corrected JSON
   - Record the repair attempt in `model_run_attempts`.
   - Preserve the original failure for audit.

4. Add no-network verification scripts.
   - `packages/ai/src/__verify__/macro-relationship-schema.ts`
   - `packages/ai/src/__verify__/coverage-audit-schema.ts`
   - Include wrapper, alias, empty-array, malformed-enum, and unrecoverable
     cases.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Macro schema accepts reasonable wrappers and aliases.
- Coverage schema accepts reasonable wrappers and aliases.
- Invalid support claim IDs still fail.
- A bad single coverage finding does not discard otherwise valid findings.
- Verification scripts pass locally.

## Phase 2: Fix Follow-Up Orchestration Order

### Problem

`source-outline` currently triggers lens fan-out, macro extraction, and coverage
audit at roughly the same time. Macro and coverage therefore do not wait for
lens results. Even a successful macro pass can miss the richer post-lens claim
set.

Relevant files:

- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-outline.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/macro-relationship-extraction.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-coverage-audit.ts`

### Tasks

1. Add a `document-macro-finalize` task.
   - Payload:
     - `documentId`
     - `sourceOutlineId`
     - optional `expectedLensRunCount`
   - Responsibilities:
     - Detect whether all lens runs for the outline have completed, failed, or
       timed out.
     - Trigger macro relationship extraction after lens fan-out settles.
     - Trigger coverage audit after macro extraction completes or reaches a
       terminal failure.

2. Stop `source-outline` from directly triggering macro and coverage.
   - It should trigger lens fan-out and then the finalizer.
   - If zero lenses are triggered, finalizer can run immediately.
   - If lenses are triggered, finalizer should use either delayed retry or DB
     state to wait.

3. Track lens follow-up state.
   - Preferred: add a small `document_lens_runs` or `source_outline_followups`
     table.
   - Minimum viable: use `job_runs` keyed by `sourceOutlineId`, but this is
     less explicit.
   - Store:
     - `source_outline_id`
     - `source_group_id`
     - `lens`
     - `trigger_run_id`
     - `status`
     - `claims_promoted`
     - `duplicates_appended`
     - `finished_at`

4. Make coverage audit run last.
   - Coverage should see:
     - source outline
     - broad extraction claims
     - lens extraction claims
     - macro relationships if they exist
     - macro failure metadata if macro extraction failed

### Acceptance Criteria

- Macro relationship extraction starts only after lens fan-out reaches terminal
  state.
- Coverage audit starts only after macro relationship extraction succeeds or
  fails terminally.
- The finalizer is idempotent and safe to retry.
- Rerun artifacts show the full ordered sequence.

## Phase 3: Add Deterministic Visual Claim-Kind Discipline

### Problem

The rerun produced 127 `policy` claims from a visual flowchart. The prompt says
a bare arrow is not policy, but the model still upgraded procedural-looking
sequence into official policy.

Relevant files:

- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
- `packages/ai/src/prompts/extraction-system.ts`

### Tasks

1. Add a deterministic visual policy gate.
   - Apply when:
     - source is image/visual
     - visual graph mode is detected
     - model emits `claimKind = policy`
   - Preserve `policy` only when the exact quote or nearby rendering contains
     explicit policy evidence.

2. Define explicit policy evidence terms.
   - Examples:
     - `policy`
     - `SOP`
     - `standard operating`
     - `required`
     - `must`
     - `shall`
     - `authorization`
     - `approval required`
     - `compliance`
   - A box named `Approval` can support approval-gate meaning, but not
     automatically `policy`.

3. Downgrade unsupported visual policy labels.
   - If visual arrow/edge lacks explicit policy evidence:
     - set `claimKind = observed_practice` or `uncertain`
     - cap `claimKindConfidence` at 6 for observed practice
     - cap `claimKindConfidence` at 5 for uncertain
   - Store metadata:
     - `claimKindOriginal`
     - `claimKindDowngradeReason`
     - `visualPolicyGateApplied: true`

4. Apply the same gate in broad document ingestion and document lens extraction.

5. Add tests.
   - Bare arrow fixture should not produce policy.
   - Explicit `required approval` fixture may produce policy.
   - `Approval` box alone should not force policy.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Visual diagram reruns do not generate large numbers of `policy` claims unless
  the source text visibly supports them.
- Downgrade decisions are visible in metadata.
- Admin reviewers can still revise kind manually.

## Phase 4: Deduplicate Before Promotion

### Problem

Lens fan-out promoted many repeated claims. The comparison showed exact summary
duplicates and likely near-duplicates. Deduplication must happen before claims
hit the review queue.

Relevant files:

- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
- `packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/promote-candidate.ts`
- candidate hash helpers under `packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/`

### Tasks

1. Add same-document exact summary dedup.
   - Normalize summary:
     - lowercase
     - trim punctuation
     - collapse whitespace
   - Within same document and same source batch family, keep one candidate.

2. Add same-quote plus same-claim-type dedup.
   - If candidates share:
     - document ID
     - normalized exact quote
     - claim type
   - Keep best candidate by confidence/impact or earliest broad extraction
     candidate.

3. Add cross-type visual duplicate resolution.
   - If same quote produces multiple claim types:
     - prefer `exception_rule` for conditional branch
     - prefer `dependency` for simple sequence
     - prefer `process_rule` only when source states an actual rule
   - Drop or mark weaker variants as duplicates.

4. Add bounded fuzzy dedup.
   - Scope only:
     - same document
     - same source chunk
     - same or highly similar quote
   - Use token/Jaccard similarity first.
   - Consider embeddings only inside a small candidate set.

5. Record duplicate outcomes.
   - In validation results:
     - `duplicate_same_summary`
     - `duplicate_same_quote_same_type`
     - `duplicate_cross_type_visual_edge`
     - `near_duplicate_same_source`
   - In job output:
     - `duplicatesCollapsedBeforePromotion`
     - `crossTypeDuplicatesResolved`

### Acceptance Criteria

- Lens fan-out no longer multiplies repeated claims for the same visual edge.
- Duplicate candidates remain auditable.
- Existing R5 quote validation remains unchanged.

## Phase 5: Make Lens Fan-Out Focused

### Problem

The rerun's lens output behaved like repeated broad extraction. Four handoff
lens runs promoted 249 lens claims. Lens passes need to target missing coverage,
not restate broad process edges.

Relevant files:

- `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-outline.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-lens-extraction.ts`
- `apps/workers/src/lib/document-lens-budget.ts`

### Tasks

1. Pass existing claims into lens prompts.
   - Include broad extraction claims for the same document/group.
   - Include earlier lens claims for the same outline.
   - Prompt instruction:
     - Do not restate any listed claims.
     - Only extract missing facts for this lens.

2. Limit lens input to the group.
   - Use `source_group_items` to select group-specific chunks.
   - If every group references all chunks, improve source-outline grouping.
   - Avoid passing the full visual rendering to every lens unless necessary.

3. Enforce lens diversity.
   - Do not let all selected lens runs be `handoffs`.
   - Prefer a mix:
     - `handoffs`
     - `exceptions_and_workarounds`
     - `dependencies_and_sequence`
     - `systems_and_data_entry`
   - No more than one lens per group by default.

4. Add per-lens extraction criteria.
   - `handoffs`: only ownership/role handoffs.
   - `exceptions_and_workarounds`: only branches, loops, fail/pass paths.
   - `systems_and_data_entry`: only system touchpoints/data movement.
   - `dependencies_and_sequence`: only missing sequence dependencies.

5. Skip covered groups.
   - Before triggering a lens, compare source group against existing claims.
   - Skip if broad extraction already covers the group.
   - Store skip reason in outline budget metadata.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Lens fan-out increases coverage without producing a claim explosion.
- Job output shows skipped groups and reasons.
- The same relationship is not extracted by broad, handoff, and dependency
  passes as separate claims.

## Phase 6: Add Durable Visual Context

### Problem

The system still treats a visual graph as anonymous text. Without durable
relationship IDs, each worker can rediscover the same edge with different words.

Relevant files:

- `apps/workers/src/trigger/document-ingestion.ts`
- `packages/ai/src/prompts/visual-understanding.ts`
- `packages/db/src/schema.ts`
- new migration under `packages/db/migrations/sql/`

### Tasks

1. Add `document_visual_contexts`.
   - Suggested columns:
     - `id`
     - `document_id`
     - `model_run_id`
     - `context_pack_id`
     - `prompt_version`
     - `artifact_type`
     - `artifact_type_confidence`
     - `visual_grammar_json`
     - `elements_json`
     - `relationships_json`
     - `regions_json`
     - `uncertainties_json`
     - `final_rendering_text`
     - `status`
     - `created_at`

2. Add stable relationship IDs.
   - Each relationship should contain:
     - `relationshipId`
     - source element ID
     - target element ID
     - label/condition
     - inferred relationship type
     - owner/group/region
     - direction confidence
     - uncertainty notes
     - rendering line/span

3. Persist final rendering as chunks.
   - Existing quote validation still uses `document_chunks.raw_text`.
   - Chunk metadata should record:
     - `visualContextId`
     - relationship IDs covered by the chunk
     - element IDs covered by the chunk

4. Preserve visual IDs on candidates and claims.
   - Candidate metadata:
     - `visualContextId`
     - `visualRelationshipIds`
     - `visualElementIds`
     - `visualRegionIds`
   - Claim metadata after promotion should preserve the same IDs.

5. Dedup by relationship ID.
   - Default rule:
     - one distinct visual relationship generally produces one claim.
   - Multiple claims from one relationship require distinct claim type and
     distinct supported meaning.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Every visual claim can be traced to both a quote span and a visual
  relationship ID.
- Deduplication can identify the same edge across broad and lens passes.
- Source outline can consume structured visual context instead of inferring all
  visual structure from prose.

## Phase 7: Add Visual Faithfulness Validation

### Problem

Current validation proves a claim quotes the transcription. It does not prove
the transcription matches the image.

### Tasks

1. Add deterministic visual context validation.
   - Every relationship references existing element IDs.
   - Every relationship has a rendering span.
   - No duplicate relationship IDs.
   - Direction confidence is present.
   - Uncertainty notes exist when confidence is low.
   - Final rendering is non-empty and chunkable.

2. Add optional self-verification.
   - Input:
     - original image
     - final rendering
     - structured visual context
   - Ask only:
     - missing elements
     - wrong endpoints
     - wrong direction
     - wrong grouping/owner
     - unsupported relationship
     - dropped labels
   - Do not let self-verification directly create approved facts.

3. Add eval fixtures.
   - POP flow image.
   - Org chart.
   - Table screenshot.
   - App screenshot/form.
   - Non-swimlane process diagram.

4. Add metrics.
   - Element recall/precision.
   - Relationship recall/precision.
   - Endpoint accuracy.
   - Direction accuracy.
   - Owner/group accuracy.
   - Branch label accuracy.
   - Duplicate ratio.
   - Claim kind error rate.
   - Quote validation pass rate.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Prompt/model changes can be compared against ground truth.
- Future model swaps cannot silently regress visual faithfulness.

## Phase 8: Improve Macro Relationship Quality

### Problem

Even after schema parsing is fixed, macro extraction needs curated support
claims and better prompts to avoid weak summaries.

### Tasks

1. Build support claim clusters.
   - Approval gates.
   - Rework loops.
   - Buyer/customer branches.
   - Licensor branches.
   - System/data handoffs.
   - Production/shipping path.

2. Feed macro extraction curated clusters, not arbitrary top 40 claims.

3. Ask for relationship types tied to graph structure.
   - Sequence backbone.
   - Approval gate.
   - Exception path.
   - Cross-role handoff chain.
   - System handoff chain.

4. Require minimum support.
   - Usually 2+ support claims.
   - Allow one support claim only for a simple source-local sequence backbone
     when evidence is unambiguous.

5. Add macro duplicate prevention.
   - Same outline + relationship type + similar summary should not create
     multiple rows.

6. Improve prompt examples.
   - Include valid output examples.
   - Include `{"relationships":[]}` no-op example.
   - Emphasize support claim IDs exactly as provided.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Process diagrams with hundreds of claims produce useful macro relationships.
- Macro rows are pending/reviewable, not silently absent.
- Weak or duplicate macro summaries are filtered before persistence.

## Phase 9: Make Coverage Audit A Real Safety Net

### Problem

When macro extraction fails or produces nothing, coverage audit should create
actionable findings. It should not also silently fail or return zero rows.

### Tasks

1. Compare outline groups against claims.
   - For every source group:
     - count supporting claims.
     - count macro relationships.
     - detect missing owner/branch/stage coverage.

2. Add deterministic fallback findings.
   - If model coverage audit fails:
     - create an admin-only finding that coverage audit failed.
   - If macro relationships are zero for a rich outline:
     - create `low_claim_density` or `macro_only_source` as appropriate.

3. Consider adding internal finding types.
   - `macro_relationship_generation_failed`
   - `duplicate_atomic_volume`
   - `policy_kind_overuse`

4. Add coverage metrics to job output.
   - Groups checked.
   - Claims per group.
   - Relationships per group.
   - Findings generated.
   - Model findings dropped during schema normalization.

### Acceptance Criteria

- A rich visual outline with no macro relationships produces at least one
  coverage finding.
- Coverage audit failure is visible in admin artifacts and job output.

## Phase 10: Harden Re-Evaluation Tooling

### Problem

`scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs` initially failed because macro/source-outline
refs still pointed at chunks. The script must stay current with the macro
schema.

### Tasks

1. Keep source-outline cleanup in the guarded script.
   - Delete document-tied:
     - `source_coverage_findings`
     - `source_group_items`
     - `source_groups`
     - `source_outline_source_refs`
     - `source_outline_sources`
     - `source_outlines`

2. Include macro cleanup.
   - Delete document/claim/outline-tied:
     - `macro_relationship_review_events`
     - `macro_relationship_claims`
     - `macro_relationship_sources`
     - `macro_relationships`

3. Add extra blockers.
   - Approved macro relationships tied to target claims.
   - Macro relationships consumed by Brain/chat once such lineage exists.

4. Expand dry-run output.
   - Print:
     - chunks
     - claims
     - candidates
     - batches
     - outlines
     - groups
     - macro relationships
     - coverage findings

5. Add a smoke fixture or script.
   - Document with chunks plus source outline refs should reset without FK
     errors.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Re-evaluation does not FK fail when source outlines exist.
- Dry run makes all affected artifact classes visible.
- Approved macro knowledge cannot be accidentally deleted.

## Phase 11: Improve Artifact And Metrics Export

### Problem

The comparison artifact was useful, but some metrics were hard to interpret.
Historical job rows mixed with current rerun rows, and quote-overlap metrics
need to read the exact evidence column reliably.

### Tasks

1. Fix quote extraction in export helper.
   - Use actual `claim_evidence` quote field names.
   - Verify normalized quote overlap is measuring real quote text.

2. Separate run windows.
   - Export:
     - all historical rows
     - current rerun rows only
   - Use explicit file names:
     - `rerun-job-outputs-after-rerun.json`
     - `historical-job-outputs.json`

3. Add model/prompt metadata.
   - Vision route.
   - Vision prompt version.
   - Extraction route.
   - Extraction prompt version.
   - Source outline prompt version.
   - Macro prompt version.
   - Coverage prompt version.

4. Add quality metrics.
   - Claims per visual relationship once available.
   - Duplicate collapsed count.
   - Policy downgrade count.
   - Macro rows persisted.
   - Coverage findings persisted.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Comparison summaries distinguish real claim loss from changed rendering
  wording.
- Rerun artifacts are reproducible without manual DB archaeology.

## Phase 12: Rerun Gate And Rollout

### Pre-Rerun Gates

Run before production-like resets:

- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/visual-understanding-prompt.ts`
- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/source-outline-schema.ts`
- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/macro-relationship-schema.ts`
- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai exec tsx src/__verify__/coverage-audit-schema.ts`
- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/ai run typecheck`
- `corepack pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run typecheck`

### Production-Like Rerun Thresholds

For this POP flow image:

- New claim count should not rise more than 20 percent unless duplicate collapse
  explains the increase.
- Visual `policy` claims should be rare and justified by explicit source text.
- Macro relationships should be nonzero.
- Coverage findings should be nonzero if macro relationships are zero.
- Duplicate summary groups should be near zero.
- Lens claims should add missing coverage, not restate broad extraction.

### Rollout Order

1. Tolerant macro/coverage schemas and repair retry.
2. Staged finalizer after lens fan-out.
3. Deterministic visual policy downgrade.
4. Pre-promotion deduplication.
5. Focused lens prompts and budget diversity.
6. Durable visual context and relationship IDs.
7. Visual faithfulness eval harness.
8. Rerun the POP flow document and compare again.

## Final Diagnosis

The visual prompt gave the system a better textual substrate, but downstream
workers still treated that substrate as anonymous prose. The pipeline therefore
created more atomic claims instead of stable process understanding.

The durable fix is to give visual relationships identity, make every extraction
pass respect that identity, and run macro/coverage only after the atomic layer
has settled.

+Exact next action:
+- Start with `fix_extraction.md` Phase 1 and Phase 2: tolerant macro/coverage
+  schemas plus repair retry, then staged finalization after lens fan-out. Do not
+  run another production-like reset of the POP flow document until those two
+  phases pass local verification.
+
+---
 
 ## Macro understanding implementation — 2026-07-02

 ---
 
+## Visual/macro extraction recovery plan — 2026-07-03
+
+Status:
+open. The 2026-07-03 rerun of `Pop Creations Flow 12112025 (1).png`
+(`documents.id=9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a`) showed that the new
+visual prompt and lens fan-out increased atomic claim volume but did not yet
+produce holistic macro understanding.
+
+Read next:
+- `fix_extraction.md` — comprehensive plan to stabilize macro/coverage schema
+  parsing, stage follow-up orchestration after lens fan-out, enforce visual
+  claim-kind discipline, deduplicate before promotion, focus lens fan-out, add
+  durable visual relationship identity, add visual faithfulness evals, harden
+  re-evaluation tooling, and improve rerun artifacts.
+
+Evidence from the rerun:
+- Artifact folder:
+  `.cache/macro-comparison/9d09fa89-3a46-465e-a98b-837287c9e22a/rerun-20260703T022732Z/`
+- Old live snapshot before reset: 242 claims.
+- New live snapshot after rerun: 307 claims.
+- New source outlines: 1.
+- New macro relationships: 0.
+- New coverage findings: 0.
+- 127 new claims were labeled `policy`, too high for a visual process map
+  without explicit policy/SOP language.
+- Comparison verdict: `mostly_increased_atomic_volume`.
+
+Known blockers found during the rerun:
+- `source-outline` triggered lens fan-out, macro relationship extraction, and
+  coverage audit concurrently; macro/coverage should wait until lens fan-out
+  settles.
+- Macro relationship extraction and coverage audit first hit SQL
+  `SELECT DISTINCT`/`ORDER BY` projection issues. Local fixes were made in
+  `apps/workers/src/trigger/macro-relationship-extraction.ts` and
+  `apps/workers/src/trigger/source-coverage-audit.ts`, then deployed as
+  Trigger worker `20260703.2`.
+- After the SQL fix, both follow-ups still failed structured-output validation
+  against the current `general` route (`qwen_3_7_max_extraction_eval`):
+  macro output missed top-level `relationships`; coverage output used malformed
+  finding fields.
+- `scripts/reevaluate-document.mjs` initially failed because source-outline refs
+  still pointed at chunks. It was locally patched to delete document-tied
+  source-outline/macro artifacts before chunk deletion.
+
+Exact next action:
+- Start with `fix_extraction.md` Phase 1 and Phase 2: tolerant macro/coverage
+  schemas plus repair retry, then staged finalization after lens fan-out. Do not
+  run another production-like reset of the POP flow document until those two
+  phases pass local verification.
+
+---
+
